title: Gujarati Shrikhand

ingredients.name: full-fat curd (set overnight in the fridge), strained to make hung curd
visual_checks: straining 750 ml full-fat curd for 6–8 hours yields about 300 g hung curd (chakka)
ingredients.notes: straining 750 ml full-fat curd for 6–8 hours yields about 300 g hung curd (chakka)

subs.name: 500 g ready-made Greek yogurt strained for 1 hour — shortcut that saves overnight straining
subs.notes: result is slightly less firm but acceptable

ingredients.name: powdered sugar (caster or icing sugar)
visual_checks: adjust to taste — Gujarati shrikhand is very sweet
ingredients.notes: adjust to taste — Gujarati shrikhand is very sweet

subs.name: a tiny drop of yellow food colour + 0.25 tsp vanilla essence for a milder flavour
cashews halved or any nut you have; the garnish is flexible

ingredients.name: rose water
visual_checks: adds a floral note; skip if you prefer a pure cardamom-saffron flavour
ingredients.notes: adds a floral note; skip if you prefer a pure cardamom-saffron flavour

steps.text: The night before (or at least 6 hours ahead), line a large colander with a clean muslin cloth or several layers of cheesecloth. Pour in the curd, tie the corners of the cloth and hang it over a bowl in the refrigerator. Let it drain until you have a dense, clay-like mass (chakka) and at least 400 ml of whey has dripped out.
Soak the saffron strands in 2 tbsp of warm milk for 10 minutes until the milk turns a deep golden yellow. Freshly crack and powder the cardamom seeds with a pinch of sugar to keep them from clumping.

Transfer the hung curd to a mixing bowl. Add powdered sugar, cardamom powder and the saffron-milk. Beat vigorously with a whisk or electric beater for 2–3 minutes until the mixture is completely smooth, light and creamy with no lumps of curd visible.
Add rose water if using and fold in gently. Taste — the shrikhand should be very sweet with a pronounced cardamom-saffron fragrance. Add more sugar or cardamom as needed.

Transfer to a serving bowl or individual cups, cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving. Garnish generously with sliced pistachios and almonds just before bringing to the table. Serve alongside hot puris for the classic combination.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → 500 g ready-made Greek yogurt strained for 1 hour — shortcut that saves overnight straining (result is slightly less firm but acceptable); saffron → a tiny drop of yellow food colour + 0.25 tsp vanilla essence for a milder flavour; pistachio → cashews halved or any nut you have; the garnish is flexible
